mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during monetary tightening phases, or 5-day forward returns show zero correlation with INDPRO momentum across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive relationship with 20-day SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, accelerating inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations to produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL breaches 235.0, or inflation spikes fail to trigger subsequent equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining real GDP growth and rising unemployment signals a broad economic slowdown, which reduces corporate profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration of GDPC1 and UNRATE fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDPC1 remains positive despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and stable retail sales indicates supply-side efficiency gains without demand overheating, which boosts corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive accuracy when retail sales decline alongside industrial production, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with the specified divergence pattern.
